blockingIssues.description: Resume persistence is not crash-safe. Completed checkpoint state is only saved at end-of-run (and on budget-exceeded exit), so a process crash mid-run can lose completed checkpoints and prevent true resume-from-next-incomplete behavior.
blockingIssues.evidence: run_two_agent loop starts at line 607; checkpoint metrics are accumulated in memory and only persisted at lines 626 and 680.

blockingIssues.description: Concurrent Docker isolation is not wired into the normal two-agent execution path. run_slop_code injects SCBENCH_RUN_ID, but run_two_agent never calls run_slop_code, so per-run container isolation is not enforced during runner execution.
blockingIssues.evidence: run_slop_code is defined at line 481 and referenced only in tests (tests/runner/test_isolation.py:319 and :348), not in runner runtime code.

nonBlockingIssues.description: load_resume_state is currently unused, which increases maintenance cost and test burden without contributing to runtime behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: python-worker procedure is out of sync with mission reality. Skill mandates tests-first TDD and full-repo validators, while mission AGENTS.md requires scoped validators due upstream failures; transcript shows implementation edits before tests and scoped validation commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md steps 4 and 6; mission AGENTS.md 'Validator Scope'; worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton for session a4264fcb-5b0e-48ed-b08e-dce381b0a53a.

commands: grep-equivalent via Grep tool for run_slop_code(, SCBENCH_RUN_ID, state.save_results(), and call sites

summary: Review failed. Resume behavior is not crash-resilient because progress is not persisted after each completed checkpoint, and Docker isolation wiring is not active in the normal runner execution path.
